summary: Joel Spolsky puts out a call to readers for firsthand experiences with offshore software development, specifically US-India development teams. He notes the common narrative that India offers low salaries and high skills, but says he hasn't seen a rigorous general analysis of whether it actually works. Having worked on such a team himself, he wants honest accounts from both sides to write a thorough article on the topic.
key_points: Offshore development (particularly US-India) was becoming a hot topic in 2000, driven by the salary differential and available talent
Joel acknowledges the lack of rigorous, honest analysis beyond rumors and anecdotes about whether offshoring actually delivers on its promises
He explicitly asks for brutal honesty about costs, savings, problems, and successes from people with direct experience
money_quotes: I've heard a lot of rumours, and I've worked on one such team myself, but I've never seen a more general analysis of whether it can be made to work.
This site is not known for pulling punches.
key_insight: Despite growing enthusiasm for offshore software development, there was a notable absence of honest, experience-based analysis of whether it actually delivers on its promises of cost savings and quality.
